Package com.stardevllc.starlib.time

Enum Class TimeUnit

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Enum<TimeUnit>
com.stardevllc.starlib.time.TimeUnit
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Comparable<TimeUnit>, Constable
public enum TimeUnit extends Enum<TimeUnit>
An enum utility to make working with times easier. Yes, there is a Java class for this, but This was to make it easier to parse times in a Minecraft context, plus the java TimeUnit does not have weeks, months or years

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested classes/interfaces inherited from class java.lang.Enum

    Enum Constant
    Description
    DAYS
     
    HOURS
     
    MILLISECONDS
     
    MINUTES
     
    MONTHS
    The MONTHS value is based on a 30.41666667-day month.
    SECONDS
     
    TICKS
     
    WEEKS
    The WEEKS value is based on a 7.604166666666667-day week.
    YEARS
    The YEARS value is based on a 365-day year.

    Converts the amount to the days equivalent, Note: Converting from lower units to higher units will loose precision

  • Enum Constant Details

    • MILLISECONDS

      public static final TimeUnit MILLISECONDS

    • TICKS

      public static final TimeUnit TICKS

    • SECONDS

      public static final TimeUnit SECONDS

    • MINUTES

      public static final TimeUnit MINUTES

    • HOURS

      public static final TimeUnit HOURS

    • DAYS

      public static final TimeUnit DAYS

    • WEEKS

      public static final TimeUnit WEEKS
      The WEEKS value is based on a 7.604166666666667-day week. This is the average weeks in a month based on the below month value

    • MONTHS

      public static final TimeUnit MONTHS
      The MONTHS value is based on a 30.41666667-day month. This is the average days in a month based on a 365 day year

    • YEARS

      public static final TimeUnit YEARS
      The YEARS value is based on a 365-day year. If another day count is needed, convert from the DAYS enum value.

  • Method Details

    • values

      public static TimeUnit[] values()
      Returns an array containing the constants of this enum class, in the order they are declared.
      Returns:
      an array containing the constants of this enum class, in the order they are declared

    • valueOf

      public static TimeUnit valueOf(String name)
      Returns the enum constant of this class with the specified name. The string must match exactly an identifier used to declare an enum constant in this class. (Extraneous whitespace characters are not permitted.)
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the enum constant to be returned.
      Returns:
      the enum constant with the specified name
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if this enum class has no constant with the specified name
      NullPointerException - if the argument is null

    • getName

      public String getName()
      Returns:
      The primary name of the TimeUnit. This is taken from the lowercase name() value

    • getAliases

      public String[] getAliases()
      Returns:
      All aliases of the TimeUnit. This can be null

    • fromMillis

      public long fromMillis(long milliseconds)
      Converts the time from milliseconds to this unit. Note Precision is lost
      Parameters:
      milliseconds - The time in milliseconds
      Returns:
      The amount of time represented by this unit

    • toMillis

      public long toMillis(long duration)
      Converts the amount to the millisecond equivalent
      Parameters:
      duration - The duration to convert in this Unit
      Returns:
      The time in milliseconds

    • toTicks

      public long toTicks(long duration)
      Converts the amount to the ticks equivalent, Note: Converting from lower units to higher units will loose precision
      Parameters:
      duration - The duration to convert in this Unit
      Returns:
      The time in Ticks

    • toSeconds

      public long toSeconds(long duration)
      Converts the amount to the seconds equivalent, Note: Converting from lower units to higher units will loose precision
      Parameters:
      duration - The duration to convert in this Unit
      Returns:
      The time in seconds

    • toMinutes

      public long toMinutes(long duration)
      Converts the amount to the minutes equivalent, Note: Converting from lower units to higher units will loose precision
      Parameters:
      duration - The duration to convert in this Unit
      Returns:
      The time in minutes

    • toHours

      public long toHours(long duration)
      Converts the amount to the hours equivalent, Note: Converting from lower units to higher units will loose precision
      Parameters:
      duration - The duration to convert in this Unit
      Returns:
      The time in hours

    • toDays

      public long toDays(long duration)
      Converts the amount to the days equivalent, Note: Converting from lower units to higher units will loose precision
      Parameters:
      duration - The duration to convert in this Unit
      Returns:
      The time in days

    • toWeeks

      public long toWeeks(long duration)
      Converts the amount to the weeks equivalent, Note: Converting from lower units to higher units will loose precision
      Parameters:
      duration - The duration to convert in this Unit
      Returns:
      The time in weeks

    • toMonths

      public long toMonths(long duration)
      Converts the amount to the months equivalent, Note: Converting from lower units to higher units will loose precision
      Parameters:
      duration - The duration to convert in this Unit
      Returns:
      The time in months

    • toYears

      public long toYears(long duration)
      Converts the amount to the years equivalent, Note: Converting from lower units to higher units will loose precision
      Parameters:
      duration - The duration to convert in this Unit
      Returns:
      The time in years

    • getMsPerUnit

      public long getMsPerUnit()
      Returns:
      The amount of milliseconds per single unit that this TimeUnit represents.

    • matchUnit

      public static TimeUnit matchUnit(String unitString)
      This matches the unit based on the name and the aliases
      Parameters:
      unitString - The string to match
      Returns:
      The unit that matches or null if it does not
